claim.statement: For irreversible, high-severity actions with no undo (delete an account, drop a database, permanently delete a project), add deliberate friction such as a type-to-confirm step (typing the resource name or the word 'delete') so the action cannot be triggered by a single reflexive click.

limitations: Friction is justified only for genuinely irreversible/critical actions; overuse trains users to ignore it

do: Use type-to-confirm (resource name or 'delete') for irreversible actions
Group irreversible controls in a clearly marked danger zone
Keep the confirm action disabled until the typed value matches
avoid: A single-click delete for irreversible operations
Applying heavy friction to routine reversible actions

strategies: gate confirm button on exact match
